content.full_text:
CITY OF SAN JOSE

801 NORTH FIRST STREET
SAN JOSE, CALIFORNIA 95110

OCT 0 3 1992

BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION

POLICE AND FIRE DEPARTMENT
RETIREMENT PLAN

(408) 277-5137

5 October 1992

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel - FPPC
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

Enclosed please find a copy of an earlier letter dated
September 25, 1992, regarding a potential conflict. Since
then, I have received a brochure from SEI that described the
Public Advisory Board's functions. (A copy of that brochure
is enclosed). There appears to be a great deal more emphasis
on trustee education then I originally understood. It also
appears that the intent for dialogue amount presenters and
attendees is for the benefit of all parties participating.

I hope this additional information proves helpful. Thank
you.

Sincerely,

Paul A. Wysocki, Chair
Police & Fire Retirement Board

Enclosures

cc: Susan Devencenzi, Counsel to Retirement Board

PAW:daw

Public Advisory Board

SEI Capital Resources
Office Locations:

Corporate
Headquarters
Boston, MA
(617) 254-1000

Atlanta, GA
(404) 955-1432

Austin, TX
(512) 732-7100

Chicago, IL
(312) 930-7500

Cleveland, OH
(216) 781-3135

Houston, TX
(713) 623-8484

Kansas City, CA
(913) 362-4946

Los Angeles, CA
(213) 607-5300

San Francisco, CA
(415) 627-1900

Halifax, Nova Scotia
(902) 425-0163

London, England
(011) 44-229-2010

Montreal, Quebec
(514) 844-1038

Toronto, Ontario
(416) 598-9000

Vancouver, BC
(604) 688-1228


SEI Capital Resources


You're invited to participate in a shared learning experience.

The SEI Capital Resources Public Advisory Board has been established to provide a learning forum for our public plan sponsors and SEI management. As an extension of our commitment to an effective, working partnership, it is our intent that this forum be mutually beneficial. You will gain practical information that can assist you in planning and decision-making; and we will gain insight to help us provide you with superior investment solutions.

As a board member, you will have the opportunity to exchange information with your peers and with prominent industry speakers.

You will also be able to discuss issues and vital concerns firsthand with us. And your input will have a direct impact on the design and development of our products and services.

The agenda.

Public Advisory Board meetings will be held twice a year, in spring and fall, in an attractive location. Naturally, we will pay for travel and accommodations.

Each meeting will feature well-known investment experts who will focus on important plan management and investment issues. These meetings will also include input sessions with you, our clients, to hear your concerns and garner your thoughts on current topics of interest.

As a member of our board, you may also be called on from time to time between our scheduled meetings to offer comments on our continuing product and service development efforts.

An important communications tool.

Indeed, we consider our clients an extremely valuable resource for new ideas. And we believe that as a Public Advisory Board member, you will find attendance at our board meetings enhances your plan management and investment knowledge, as well as our ability to provide you with the best investment solutions.

Please join us.

CITY OF SAN JOSE

801 NORTH FIRST STREET
SAN JOSE, CALIFORNIA 95110

SEP 28 1992

BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION

POLICE AND FIRE DEPARTMENT
RETIREMENT PLAN

(408) 277-5137

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel - FPPC
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

RE: Potential Conflict

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

I am writing to you at the suggestion of John Wallace of your
office following a telephone conversation I had with him on
September 15. I was appointed in November 1990 as a trustee
to the San Jose Police and Fire Department Retirement Board
as the San Jose Civil Service Commission representative.
Earlier this year, I received an invitation to join a newly
formed Public Advisory Board for SEI Capital Resources which
provides advisory services to the Retirement Board in the
area of rating the performance of the various money managers
who invest funds on behalf of the system.

As I understand, the Public Advisory Board is comprised of
other public fund trustees and is charged with providing
relevant advice to SEI so as to better serve their clientele.
The advisory Board is to meet quarterly in varying locations.
Along with the position on that Board, SEI proposes to pay
travel and lodging expenses for these meetings. My purpose
in writing is to learn what the position of the FPPC is in
relation to such an arrangement. I understand that such a
decision will be issued in writing and will require 21
working days to complete.

If you need further information, please call me at (408) 275-
9211. I look forward to your timely response. Thank you.

Sincerely,

Paul Wysocki, Chairman
San Jose P&F Retirement Board

cc: Susan Devencenzi, Counsel to Retirement Board

PAW:daw
